original: Researchers announce Borazan (harder than diamonds) been developed
NEAR Shoemaker spacecraft lands in the "saddle" region of 433 Eros, becoming the first spacecraft to land on an asteroid
Comedy film "Groundhog Day" opens across the US starringBill Murrayand Andie MacDowell, directed by Harold Ramis
Naomi Uemura, Japanese adventurer and mountain climber who was the 1st to reach the North Pole solo and the 1st to raft the Amazon River solo, born in Hidaka, Empire of Japan (d. 1984)
Daytime fireball & meteorite fall seen in eastern Siberia
Finnish cross-country skier Marja-Liisa Hämäläinen wins the 5k gold medal at the Sarajevo Winter Olympics; 2nd of 3 individual medal sweep, also winning 10k and 20k events
Boston CelticBill Russellgrabs 40 rebounds to beat Philadelphia Warriors, 136-125
Soviets decides space center to be built in Baikonur, Kazakhstan
USSR launches Venera 1 toward Venus
The last Qing Emperor of China,Puyi(age 6), abdicates after losing the support of the Chinese people and thus the "mandate of heaven"
Bonnie Blairskates female world record 500m (38.69 sec)
Wayne Gretzkyscores his 153rd point of the season, breaking the NHL record
Judy Blume, American author (Are You There God? It's Me, Margaret), born in Elizabeth, New Jersey
The Beatles first NYC concerts, two shows at Carnegie Hall
Edward Hargraves and three other men discover gold at Ophir, New South Wales, beginning Australia's first gold rush
Panic in Quito, Ecuador, after "War of the Worlds" played on radio
Albert Einsteinwarns against the hydrogen bomb
Intelunveils its first graphics chip, the i740
The Lincoln Memorial design on the U.S. penny goes into circulation. It replaces the "sheaves of wheat" design
Harvard player Frederick Thayer patents baseball catcher's mask (pat # 200,358)
National Association for theAdvancement of Colored People (NAACP)forms
George Gershwin's "Rhapsody in Blue" premieres at the influential concert "Experiment in Modern Music" held by Paul Whiteman and his orchestra at Aeolian Hall, NYC
Promotional films (music videos) for the Beatles' forthcoming single "Penny Lane" and "Strawberry Fields" debut on theEd SullivanShow in the US
Scientists warn about harmful impacts on health of genetically modified (GM) food
Raymond Kurzweil, American inventor and author (Kurzweil reading machine, text-to-speech synthesis), born in New York City
Anna Pavlova, Russian prima ballerina and choreographer (Diaghilew; Dying Swan), born in St Petersburg, Russia (d. 1931)
Team Canada beats Denmark 47-0 in hockey
North and South Korea form a joint team for a table tennis competition
Jeff Rouse swims world record 50m backstroke (24.37 sec)
American James Clark runs world record marathon (2:46:52.6) in NYC
First injection of penicillin into a patient by British physician Charles Fletcher at Radcliffe Infirmary in Oxford, England
First secret demonstration of radio signals detecting aircraft byRobert Watson-Wattand Arnold Wilkins at Daventry, England
Henry Highland Garnet becomes the first African American minister to preach to the US House of Representatives, he talks about the end of slavery

KingHenry VIIIissues challenge, beginning a jousting tournament to celebrate the birth of his son Henry. Recorded in Roll of Honour manuscript which depicts trumpeter John Blanke, only identifiable black person in Tudor England.
Rock for the Rainforest benefit concert held in Beverly Hills, California; performers include:Sting,Bruce Springsteen,Herbie Hancock,Paul Simon, Don Henley, and Bruce Hornsby
Ivan Nikolayevich Anikeyev, Russian "lost" cosmonaut, born in USSR (d. 1992)
Jackie Torrence 'The Story Lady', American story teller advocate and artist, born in Chicago, Illinois (d. 2004)
Dennis Sullivan, American mathematician (2022 Abel prize for work on topography), born in Port Huron, Michigan
Battle of the Herrings fought during Hundred Years' War when French and Scottish troops unsuccessfully attack English convoy (carrying barrels of herrings) near Rouvray
Chile gains independence from Spain
Yves-Joseph de Kerguelen-Trémarec of France discovers the uninhabited Kerguelen Archipelago in the sub-Antarctic ocean
Ecuador annexes Galapagos Islands
CaptainJames Cookarrives at Queen Charlotte Sound, New Zealand, aboard HMS Resolution, on his 3rd trip to the Pacific
1st artificial ice rink in North America at Madison Square Garden, NYC
Bus boycott starts in Macon, Georgia
Festac '77, the Second World Black and African Festival of Arts and Culture, finishes in Lagos, Nigeria

A powerful winter storm blankets the Northeastern United States dumping 1 to 2 feet of snow from Washington, D.C. up to Boston, Massachusetts. The storm dumped a record 26.9 inches of snow in New York City.
Tornado strikes near York and Livingston, Alabama, with estimated Fujita scale of F4 intensity, kills 11 people
Tornado strikes Montgomery, Alabama, with estimated Fujita scale of F3 intensity, kills 26, destroys about 100 houses, two warehouses, and a freight train;
Ringo Starr's fifth All-Starr Band debuts in concert; members include: Todd Rundgren, Jack Bruce, Gary Brooker, Simon Kirke, and Timmy Cappello
Hendrik Marchant, Dutch politician (VVD), his private member's bill led to women's suffrage), born in Deventer, Netherlands (d. 1956)
Pirate Radio Free Harlem (NYC) begins transmitting
1st Lt Nancy Leftenant becomes 1st African American in army nursing corps
Cleveland (now Los Angeles) Rams granted an NFL franchise
-47°F (-44°C), Camp Clarke, Nebraska (state record)
Great airship, USS Macon lost in a storm off Point Sur, California with the loss of two lives, with 64 people saved
Kosmos 1076, 1st Soviet oceanographic satellite, launched
Vasco da Gamasets sail from Lisbon, Portugal, on his second voyage to India
Dedication ceremony for the about to be constructedLincoln Memorialin Washington, D.C.
French fashion designerChristian Diorpresents his first influential collection, named the "New Look"
